steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: ”We came to see the famous artworks inside the State Hermitage Museum in Russia” is an independent clause. “Yet” introduces a second clause: “the building itself ______ us the most.” For both clauses to be properly joined, the second must be independent as well.

steps.title: Apply the joining rule
steps.content:
A coordinating conjunction requires a finite verb in both independent clauses. “Impressed” is a past tense finite verb that satisfies this requirement.

Choices B, C, and D are non-finite forms (participle, perfect participle, and infinitive) that cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
